qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated full API access with global CORS exposes all watch data and enables unauthorized control
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes broken access control via unauthenticated API access and CORS misconfiguration, which is unrelated to the advisory's Zip Slip vulnerability in the backup restore functionality. The advisory describes path traversal via zipfile.extractall() in the restore_backup function, while this finding describes missing authentication in API endpoints and CORS wildcard configuration. These are completely different vulnerability types and code locations.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described Zip Slip vulnerability in the backup restore functionality. The advisory describes a path traversal vulnerability in the restore_backup function that uses zipfile.extractall() without validating entry paths, allowing ../ sequences to escape the extraction directory. Neither of the qualifying findings (deserialization RCE in import endpoint, unauthenticated API access with CORS) identifies this vulnerable code path or root cause.
evidence: Advisory describes Zip Slip vulnerability in backup restore at /backups/restore endpoint using zipfile.extractall() without path validation
Advisory affected code is in restore_backup function (lines 50-53) with vulnerable extractall() call
Finding 'example' describes deserialization RCE in import endpoint (src/routes/import.js, src/jobs/processImport.js, src/lib/serialization/loadArchive.js) - completely different code path
Finding 'unauthenticated-api-access-cors' describes broken access control in API authentication and CORS configuration - completely different vulnerability type and code locations
